rerun0.24.0-alpha.1

In rerun::external::re_query::range_zip

rerun::external::re_query::range_zip

Function range_zip_1x6

Source 
pub fn range_zip_1x6<Idx, IR0, R0, IO0, O0, IO1, O1, IO2, O2, IO3, O3, IO4, O4, IO5, O5>(
    r0: IR0,
    o0: IO0,
    o1: IO1,
    o2: IO2,
    o3: IO3,
    o4: IO4,
    o5: IO5,
) -> RangeZip1x6<Idx, <IR0 as IntoIterator>::IntoIter, R0, <IO0 as IntoIterator>::IntoIter, O0, <IO1 as IntoIterator>::IntoIter, O1, <IO2 as IntoIterator>::IntoIter, O2, <IO3 as IntoIterator>::IntoIter, O3, <IO4 as IntoIterator>::IntoIter, O4, <IO5 as IntoIterator>::IntoIter, O5> â“˜
where
    Idx: Ord,
    IR0: IntoIterator<Item = (Idx, R0)>,
    IO0: IntoIterator<Item = (Idx, O0)>,
    IO1: IntoIterator<Item = (Idx, O1)>,
    IO2: IntoIterator<Item = (Idx, O2)>,
    IO3: IntoIterator<Item = (Idx, O3)>,
    IO4: IntoIterator<Item = (Idx, O4)>,
    IO5: IntoIterator<Item = (Idx, O5)>,
Expand description

Returns a new RangeZip1x6 iterator.

The number of elements in a range zip iterator corresponds to the number of elements in the shortest of its required iterators (r0).

Each call to next is guaranteed to yield the next value for each required iterator, as well as the most recent index amongst all of them.

Optional iterators accumulate their state and yield their most recent value (if any), each time the required iterators fire.
